comment.body:
while the US in the right corner does its best to absorb every blow and pray its backers don’t throw in the towel. 
In the stands, the Russian and Arab delegations—who’ve been rooting for the US as their favorite—keep shouting, “Fuck, get yourself together!” Meanwhile, they’re clearly still undecided about whether to sponsor him for the upcoming season. As for Lady Europe, previously known in the tabloids as US’ sweetheart, is in a hysterical frenzy, being clearly unsure yet whose bankroll she wants to gold-dig from in the future…

comment.body: you guys can read just like I can. It was all in her post. It's still a risk, but the goalposts haven't moved. All want China on the sidelines (RU/US/SA) as a means to an end for access to tech market, etc. But, it's all a Mexican standoff to see who will gain the edge and who can cover their weaknesses/expose others the best. US QE has its risks as well which she's highlighted as it can be exploited and cause further harm to economy. The sooner they get EU onside, the more it shores up the RU/SA alliance to neuter China
